title: Recommended Deck: “Amane Kanata”
description: This is an aggressive deck that excels at downing your opponent’s holomem.Use Arts with additional life damage effects to down your opponent’s holomem and push for a decisive finish in one go.

strategy.text: In the early game, deploy Debut holomem to the stage and build up holo Power through collab while using support cards such as hSD01-016 [Harusaki Nodoka] and hBP02-084 [Mikkorone 24] to refine your hand.If you go second, use Arts from your first turn and combine them with the collab effect of hBP01-010 [Amane Kanata] to deal significant damage.In the mid game, bloom your Debut holomem into 1st holomem.Use hBP01-013 [Amane Kanata] to deal damage to your opponent’s center holomem while aiming to down them with Arts.The 2nd holomem, hBP01-014 [Amane Kanata] and hSD08-004 [Amane Kanata], shine in the late game, but their Arts require three cheer to use, so be careful with how you allocate your cheer.In the late game, make full use of the two 2nd [Amane Kanata] to down your opponent’s holomem.With hBP01-014 [Amane Kanata], aim for additional damage to your opponent’s life. With hSD08-004 [Amane Kanata], use its ability to strengthen your collab holomem and deal heavy damage.Combine them with your Oshi skill to gain the advantage in the life race.

key_cards.name: Amane Kanata【2nd】
key_cards.text: While she is in the center position, the Arts of your Debut holomem in the collab position gain +40. When hBP01-009 [Amane Kanata] collabs, it can deal 80 damage to your opponent’s center holomem with just one white cheer. Deal heavy damage with minimal cheer, and prepare your next holomem in the back position to maintain your momentum.

key_cards.name: Amane Kanata【Debut】
key_cards.text: She can only use Arts on your opponent’s center holomem, but even so, she is an extremely powerful Debut holomem who can deal 40 damage with just one white cheer.By combining her with hBP01-114 [Stone Axe], hBP02-079 [Explosion Magic], and other holomem abilities, that damage can increase two to three times over. Catch your opponent off guard and deliver a devastating blow.

strategy.text: In the early game, place the Debut holomem [Anya Melfissa] and [Pavolia Reine] on the stage, and prepare to bloom them into their 1st holomem on the following turn.By using [Two-Tone PC], you can add both of their 1st holomem from your deck to your hand, so make sure to use it proactively when the timing allows.The bloom effect of hBP04-027 [Pavolia Reine] allows you to send cheers to [Anya Melfissa]. Distribute cheers in a balanced way so that both your center holomem and your collab holomem can use their Arts.In the mid game, use support cards such as hSD01-016 [Harusaki Nodoka] and hSD01-017 [Manager-chan] to strengthen your hand and stage.In addition, use the Oshi skill of hBP04-007 [Anya Melfissa] to add [Ancient Weapon] from your deck to your hand.[Ancient Weapon] becomes more powerful depending on the number of [Ancient Weapon] on your stage, so aim to have three to four [Ancient Weapons] on the stage by the late game.In the late game, bloom into the 2nd holomem hBP04-078 [Anya Melfissa].While hBP04-078 [Anya Melfissa] is in the center or collab position, the number of yellow cheer required to use the Arts of your center [Anya Melfissa] with [Ancient Weapon] attached is reduced by 1.If she is in both the center and collab position, the yellow cheer cost is reduced by 2, allowing you to use powerful Arts with minimal cheer.The SP Oshi skill of hBP04-007 [Anya Melfissa] allows you to send cheer from the archive to a holomem with [Ancient Weapon] attached.Use this skill when you have two hBP04-078 [Anya Melfissa] on the stage to overwhelm your opponent and take down their holomem in one decisive push.

key_cards.name: Anya Melfissa【1st】
key_cards.text: With its gift ability, when this holomem is downed, you can return one holomem from among this holomem and the holomem stacked beneath it to your hand.If [Ancient Weapon] is attached to this holomem, the holomem returned to your hand by the gift ability can be used as the recovery cost for [Ancient Weapon], making this ability useful in many situations.Until you have enough [Ancient Weapon] on your stage and are ready to bloom into your 2nd holomem, continue attaching [Ancient Weapon] and deal 60–90 damage consistently to maintain pressure.

key_cards.name: Ancient Weapon
key_cards.text: [Ancient Weapon] is a tool that increases Arts damage by +10 for each [Ancient Weapon] on your stage.Since the Arts value increases based on the number of [Ancient Weapon] on your stage, aim to place all four [Ancient Weapon] from your deck onto the stage to create an [Ancient Weapon] with the maximum Arts +40.When a holomem with [Ancient Weapon] is downed, you can archive one card from your hand instead of the [Ancient Weapon] itself to recover it. If [Ancient Weapon] is about to be archived, use an unnecessary card from your hand as a substitute.

strategy.text: In the early game, place your Debut holomem on the stage while using support cards such as hSD01-016 [Harusaki Nodoka] and hBP02-084 [Mikkorone 24] to organize your hand.hBP04-068 [Oozora Subaru] is difficult for your opponent to down early on due to her gift ability that reduces damage, allowing you to safely collab with her.In the mid game, you can either use hBP04-071 [Oozora Subaru]’s Arts to assign cheers in preparation for the late game, or use hBP04-069 [Oozora Subaru]’s high-damage Arts to aim for opponent downs. Both holomem have high HP, allowing you to use [Stone Axe] effectively.Once the Oshi skill "Mental! Physical! Passion!" becomes available, it can be used to deter your opponent’s Arts. Using this Oshi skill as a shield while making full use of her high HP forms the core of your strategy.In the late game, bloom into the 2nd holomem hBP04-072 [Oozora Subaru]. This holomem can deal massive damage depending on the number of cheers on both stages.Additionally, the SP Oshi skill "Comedic Reactions" can only be used when your life is 3 or less, but it boosts the Arts of your center holomem [Oozora Subaru] by +100, allowing you to take down most holomem in a single hit. Use these effects to simultaneously take down your opponent’s center and collab holomem and aim for victory.

key_cards.name: Oozora Subaru【1st】
key_cards.text: Its collab effect gives it a powerful ability to boost its Arts by up to +30. Since it can use its Arts with just 1 cheer, concentrating cheers on the center holomem allows it to effectively deal 50 damage to an opponent holomem with a single cheer.

key_cards.name: Subaru Duck
key_cards.text: In addition to its HP boost, if there are 10 or more cheers on both stages, its Arts is increased by +20. Being a mascot, it can be attached alongside other tools, and attaching hBP01-114 [Stone Axe] allows it to reach a maximum Arts of +40.

strategy.text: In the early game, deploy Debut holomem to the stage while using support cards like hSD01-016 [Harusaki Nodoka] and hBP02-084 [Mikkorone 24] to smooth out your hand.If you can get even a single hBP01-056 [Takane Lui] onto the stage using hBP01-104 [Normal PC] or hBP02-084 [Mikkorone 24], your plays in the mid to late game will become much more consistent.Bloom your Debut holomem into 1st holomem as the game progresses.hBP04-056 [La+ Darknesss] boasts high HP and easy-to-use Arts, making her a key powerhouse in the mid game.Use hBP04-089 [Two-Tone PC] to search for the 1st holomem of [La+ Darknesss] and [Takane Lui], and prepare for the late game by setting up turns where you can roll a die more times when you bloom into 2nd [La+ Darknesss].Once you are ready to bloom into 2nd, use your Oshi skill "Leader's Work".This allows you to draw three cards with the bloom effect "Yes My Dark!" of 2nd [La+ Darknesss], while the collab effect "Shall We Test Your Fate?" of 1st [La+ Darknesss] guarantees 30 special damage.In addition, hBP01-061 [Takane Lui] can return holomem that have been downed, and her SP Oshi skill "We Are Secret Society holoX!" also allows you to use Arts.Leverage your overwhelming hand resources to continuously down your opponent’s holomem.

key_cards.name: La+ Darknesss【2nd】
key_cards.text: She has Arts that deal greater damage the more times you roll a die.This includes die rolls not only from holomem abilities, but also from cards such as hBP02-084 [Mikkorone 24].Try combining her with various cards that allow you to roll a die to maximize her damage output.

key_cards.name: Two-Tone PC
key_cards.text: This deck is designed to shine when you combine holomem of multiple colors.While it requires two or more holomem of different colors to activate its effects, many 1st holomem have bloom effects that support your plays in subsequent turns, contributing to the overall stability and consistency of the deck.

strategy.text: In the early game, place your Debut holomem on the stage and prepare to bloom them into their 1st holomem on the following turn.By collabing with hBP04-044 [Yukihana Lamy], you can use the collab effect "Snow Flower" and the Oshi skill "I Love You" to gather hBP04-106 [Yukimin] from your deck.Since having hBP04-106 [Yukimin] attached grants bonuses, make sure to actively utilize both effects.In the mid game, use support cards such as hSD01-016 [Harusaki Nodoka] and hBP02-084 [Mikkorone 24] to organize your hand, and bloom your Debut holomem into their 1st holomem.Additionally, from the mid game onward, use hBP04-047 [Yukihana Lamy]’s collab effect "fleur" to deal damage to your opponent’s holomem and create a favorable board state.In the late game, bloom into the 2nd holomem hBP04-048 [Yukihana Lamy].Use the SP Oshi skill of hBP04-004 [Yukihana Lamy], "Vroom, Bari Bari Bari Bari", to take down multiple opponent holomem at once. This will allow you to draw a large number of cards, so aim to use it proactively and secure victory.

key_cards.name: Yukihana Lamy【2nd】
key_cards.text: With the bloom effect "Lady of Unylia", you can assign cheers to [Yukihana Lamy] with hBP04-106 [Yukimin] attached.Additionally, the Arts "May Today Be a Blessed Day" can be combined with the SP Oshi skill "Vroom, Bari Bari Bari Bari" to draw a large number of cards.

key_cards.name: Yukimin
key_cards.text: This card is designed to maximize the potential of various [Yukihana Lamy] abilities.It boosts special damage and can be searched from your deck using abilities from the early game, making it a central piece of your strategy.

strategy.text: In the early game, set up Debut holomem on the stage and prepare to bloom them into 1st holomem on the following turn.Collab with hSD05-011 [Ichijou Ririka], and by increasing your hand size with her collab effect "Leave it to me!", it becomes easier to line up Debut [Ichijou Ririka] in the back position.In the mid game, use support cards such as hSD01-016 [Harusaki Nodoka] and hBP02-084 [Mikkorone 24] to set up your hand and stage.When one of your holomem is downed by your opponent’s holomem, use the SP Oshi skill "Cute! Positive! Genius!" to gather hBP04-037 [Ichijou Ririka] and hBP04-091 [Struggle Meal] into your hand.In the late game, bloom your holomem into the 2nd holomem hBP04-037 [Ichijou Ririka], and use the bloom effect "KPG" to call a holomem into your opponent’s open collab position.Also make use of hBP04-091 [Struggle Meal] to conserve the cheer you need while dealing heavy damage and aiming for the win.

key_cards.name: Ichijou Ririka【2nd】
key_cards.text: The bloom effect "KPG" allows you to forcibly move your opponent’s holomem into the collab position, creating an opportunity to turn the tables.By using the Arts of your center holomem in combination with hBP04-091 [Struggle Meal], you can deal up to 210 damage by combining special damage while spending only a small amount of cheer.

key_cards.name: Struggle Meal
key_cards.text: This is a key card exclusive to [Ichijou Ririka] that reduces the number of cheer required to use Arts.It can be added to your hand with the SP Oshi skill “Cute! Positive! Genius!” of hBP04-003 [Ichijou Ririka], or returned to your hand with the bloom effect “Struggle Meal Chef” of hBP04-035 [Ichijou Ririka], making it a core card that sees frequent use throughout the match.

strategy.text: In the early game, aim to deploy as many Debut holomem to the stage as possible.Both the Debut and 1st holomem of [Juufuutei Raden] can use Arts with just 1 green cheer, allowing you to bring collab holomem into the performance right from the start.If you have the flexibility, use support cards such as hSD01-016 [Harusaki Nodoka] and hBP02-084 [Mikkorone 24] to further strengthen your hand and stage.In the mid game, attach hBP02-086 [holoSparkling] to a 1st [Juufuutei Raden] that you expect to bloom into a 2nd holomem.Because [Juufuutei Raden] has the "#Alcohol" tag, she can avoid holoSparkling’s drawback of "taking +10 damage", making it an excellent tool with strong synergy.If you have extra cheer available, use hSD01-019 [Amazing PC] to add 2nd holomem hBP04-025 [Juufuutei Raden] to your hand and bloom on the following turn.In the late game, make sure 2nd holomem hBP04-025 [Juufuutei Raden] stays on the stage so you can use her Arts every turn.Her Arts "Looks Like the Next Act Is Ready" allows you to reassign 1 cheer to each of two back holomem, letting you set up the holomem that will use Arts on the following turn. If you can perform [Maitake Dance] on the next turn, you will be able to use Arts smoothly, so be proactive in making use of [Maitake Dance].If you have already used [Maitake Dance] by the late game, use the SP Oshi skill "With the Extra Time, Round and Round" to recover [Maitake Dance] from the archive to your hand.Since [Maitake Dance] can also solve situations where you are just "10 damage short", be sure to save one copy for the moment when it matters most.

key_cards.name: Juufuutei Raden【1st】
key_cards.text: When she is downed during your opponent’s turn, you can reassign her cheer to another [Juufuutei Raden].Since all 1st holomem of [Juufuutei Raden] can use Arts with just 1–2 green cheer, you can keep up the pressure and continue using Arts without losing momentum by reassigning cheer as needed.If you end up with excess cheer, use it as the cost for hSD01-019 [Amazing PC] to add a 2nd holomem to your hand.

key_cards.name: Maitake Dance
key_cards.text: You can reassign cheer on the stage to [Juufuutei Raden].Since 2nd holomem hBP04-025 [Juufuutei Raden] requires 4 cheer to use her Arts, wait until you are fully prepared to put your 2nd holomem on the stage before using [Maitake Dance].The collab effect of 2nd holomem hBP04-025 [Juufuutei Raden] allows you to add [Maitake Dance] from your deck to your hand, so be sure to make active use of it when you do not have [Maitake Dance] in hand.

strategy.text: In the early game, deploy Debut holomem to the stage while using support cards such as hSD01-016 [Harusaki Nodoka] and hBP02-084 [Mikkorone 24] to smooth out your hand.If you can get even a single hBP01-048 [Kazama Iroha] onto the stage through cards like hBP01-104 [Normal PC], hBP02-084 [Mikkorone 24], or the Arts of hBP04-009 [Hakui Koyori], it will become easier to protect your center holomem from the mid game onward.Continue to bloom your Debut holomem into 1st holomem.hBP04-012 [Hakui Koyori] can search for a support card with the "#KOYO LABO" tag through her bloom effect, and when equipped with hBP04-105 [Koyori's Assistants], she allows you to draw a card when using Arts, making her extremely powerful.In addition, use hBP04-089 [Two-Tone PC] to search for hBP01-050 [Kazama Iroha], and aim for a late-game setup where you protect hBP04-013 [Hakui Koyori] with her gift ability "Bodyguard".In the late game, bloom into hBP04-013 [Hakui Koyori] and use her powerful Arts to down your opponent’s holomem.With the gift ability "I'll Never Give Up!", you can add the cards you need to your hand, and reduce the damage she takes and prevent her from being downed by using the SP Oshi skill "Assistants, mmmwah!".If hBP04-013 [Hakui Koyori] remains on the stage and can continue using her Arts over multiple turns, victory will be within reach.

key_cards.name: Hakui Koyori【1st】
key_cards.text: If hBP04-105 [Koyori's Assistants] is in the archive, you can perform more technical plays by using Arts effects to attach hBP04-105 [Koyori's Assistants] to another [Hakui Koyori], then reassigning cheer to meet the cheer requirement and use Arts.

key_cards.name: Koyori's Assistants
key_cards.text: This is a key card in the [Hakui Koyori] deck, as it fulfills the activation condition for the SP Oshi skill  "Assistants, mmmwah!" and influences the Arts effects of various [Hakui Koyori] cards.Make sure to have it attached to [Hakui Koyori] when you bloom into 2nd in the late game.

strategy.text: In the early game, set Debut holomem on the stage so you can bloom into a 1st holomem next turn. The collab with hBP03-010 Himemori Luna, along with increasing your hand size with the collab effect "Princess of Candy Kingdom", makes it easier to gather Debut holomem Himemori Luna in the back position. Furthermore, be aggressive and use the SP Oshi skill "Lu-knights, Assemble" to attach hBP03-105 Lu-knights to Himemori Luna.In the mid-game, prepare your hand and stage using support cards like hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka and hBP03-093 hololive Gen 4. If you don't have any support cards, bring hBP01-104 Normal PC or hBP02-077 Retro PC to your hand using the Oshi skill "I Know How to Use a Computew!". When your opponent's holomem uses Arts, strategically archive hBP03-105 Lu-knights to skillfully weather the attack and prevent damage to Himemori Luna.In the late game, aim to bloom your holomem into the 2nd holomem, hBP03-014 Himemori Luna. Use the bloom effect "Follow Luna~" to reattach hBP03-105 Lu-knights, which was archived in the mid-game, to Himemori Luna, further reinforcing your defense. Since hBP03-014 Himemori Luna can deal huge damage when hBP03-105 Lu-knights is attached, utilize her aggressively to secure the win.

key_cards.name: Himemori Luna【2nd】
key_cards.text: The bloom effect "Follow Luna~" allows you to attach hBP03-105 Lu-knights, previously sent to the archive, to Himemori Luna. This means you can use the ability of hBP03-105 Lu-knights freely from the early game. Furthermore, having hBP03-105 Lu-knights attached can potentially result in massive damage.

key_cards.name: Lu-knights
key_cards.text: These fans continuously protect Himemori Luna. They are the core of the deck, enabling switches between offense and defense and seeing action from the early to late game. Even when sent to the archive, the bloom effect of hBP03-014 Himemori Luna allows them to be reattached to her, so be aggressive in using them for defense.

strategy.text: In the early game, set Debut holomem on the stage so you can bloom into a 1st holomem next turn. The collab with hBP03-017 Shishiro Botan allows you to archive the top card of the cheer deck with the collab effect. Since archiving cheer enables you to effectively use the bloom effect of the 2nd holomem, hBP03-021 Shishiro Botan, in the late game, utilize this feature as aggressively as possible.In the mid-game, prepare your hand using support cards like hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka and hBP03-094 FPS Stream, then proceed to bloom your Debut holomem into 1st holomem. In addition, increase your cheer with hBP03-020 Shishiro Botan, or archive cheer while dealing damage to opponent's holomem with hBP03-019 Shishiro Botan. If hBP03-096 Rifle is attached to a holomem before they bloom, the damage from the bloom effect will be +10. If your opponent has a 1st holomem or higher in the center position, you can also try to down them with the SP Oshi skill "Sniping".In the late game, aim to bloom into the 2nd holomem, hBP03-021 Shishiro Botan. If the SP Oshi skill "Sniping" has been kept in reserve, aim for a simultaneous down of the center holomem and a collabo holomem by pairing it with hBP01-108 So, That Makes You My Enemy. When using the SP Oshi skill, combining it with hSD06-010 La+ Darknesss allows you to deal 80 damage for only 1 cheer. Time your card usage correctly to more effectively down your opponent's holomem.

key_cards.name: Shishiro Botan【1st】
key_cards.text: The bloom effect allows her to deal special damage by archiving 1 cheer. The ideal play is to use this ability to chip away at the opponent's holomem's HP, then aim for the down with the Arts of the 2nd holomem, hBP03-021 Shishiro Botan.

key_cards.name: Rifle
key_cards.text: Attach this to Shishiro Botan -- your goal should be for her to bloom to the 2nd holomem. Since the Arts of the 2nd holomem, hBP03-021 Shishiro Botan, deals special damage in addition to Arts damage, the total damage dealt will be +20, making this card extremely powerful.

strategy.text: In the early game, set Debut holomem on the stage so you can bloom into a 1st holomem next turn. Performing a collab with a holomem that has hBP03-098 Kintoki attached allows you to draw 1 card while simultaneously making your holomem more resilient due to the HP boost effect. If your opponent went first, attaching hBP03-107 35P allows you to use Arts with both your center holomem and a collabo holomem starting from your first turn.In the mid-game, prepare your hand and stage by blooming into the 1st holomem, hBP03-029 Sakura Miko, who can add support cards such as hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka and hBP03-107 35P directly to your hand. Use powerful Arts with both your 1st holomem in the center position and your collab holomem. If the cards in your hand are not ideal, use the SP Oshi skill "The Strength to not Give Up" to help improve the situation and ensure the necessary holomem are available to use Arts as you prepare for the late game.In the late game, bloom your holomem into the 2nd holomem, hBP03-030 Sakura Miko, and attempt to deal massive damage against your opponent's holomem. In addition, using the Oshi skill "Are 35P Making a Comeback?!" allows you to return archived hBP03-107 35P to your hand, resulting in an even more powerful use of Arts. If the opponent's holomem has high HP and seems difficult to down, utilize hBP01-108 So, That Makes You My Enemy or hBP03-088 Totsumachi to call out the opposing holomem and down them for the win.

key_cards.name: Sakura Miko【2nd】
key_cards.text: Although the gift Elite Gamble is highly random, a successful activation can potentially lead to massive damage, enough to down an opponent's holomem in one hit. In addition, attaching multiple hBP03-107 35P can still result in sufficient damage, even if Elite Gamble fails.

key_cards.name: 35P
key_cards.text: A fan with the ability to be treated as red cheer. Despite the drawback of allowing your opponent to draw a card when the attached holomem is downed, it can be searched for with hBP03-029 Sakura Miko. It is a key card of the deck, as many holomem in this deck have bonus effects when hBP03-107 35P is attached.

strategy.text: In the early game, use support cards such as hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka to draw cards and hBP01-104 Normal PC to put cards onto the stage so that you can set up both Debut holomem hBP03-040 Fuwawa Abyssgard and hBP03-037 Mococo Abyssgard on your stage.In the mid game, bloom your Debut holomem into a 1st holomem. hBP03-050 FUWAMOCO can bloom from either hBP03-040 Fuwawa Abyssgard or hBP03-037 Mococo Abyssgard. Sending cheer with the Arts “Twin sisters of the Demonic Guard Dogs” makes it easier for your other holomem to use their Arts.In the late game, place your 2nd holomem hBP03-043 Fuwawa Abyssgard in the center position and the Buzz holomem hBP03-039 Mococo Abyssgard in the collab position, then use their Arts. If it’s difficult to take down your opponent’s holomem, use the SP Oshi skill “BAU BAU!” to target your opponent’s back holomem and finish off their life.

key_cards.name: FUWAMOCO【1st】
key_cards.text: Even if your stage has only one of “Fuwawa Abyssgard” or “Mococo Abyssgard” among your debut holomem, via the 1st holomem hBP03-050 FUWAMOCO, you can bloom into either hBP03-039 Mococo Abyssgard or hBP03-043 Fuwawa Abyssgard, making it a versatile card.

key_cards.name: Fuwawa Abyssgard【1st】
key_cards.text: The bloom effect “Fuwa Floating with the Current” can deal special damage to back holomem, as well. When combined with the SP Oshi skill “BAU BAU!”, you can down your opponent’s back holomem while they are unprepared, disrupting your opponent’s game plan.

strategy.text: In the early game, set up your Debut holomem on the stage while organizing your hand with support cards such as hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka. Once you have 2 holo Power and can use the SP Oshi skill “Diabolical Deeds,” your opponent will find it difficult to activate their Arts. Use this ability to apply pressure on your opponent while preparing the holomem on your own stage.In the mid game, bloom your Debut holomem into a 1st holomem. hBP03-054 Tokoyami Towa and hBP03-055 Tokoyami Towa have abilities and Arts that deal special damage to your opponent. The mascot hBP03-101 Bibi and the tool hBP03-096 Rifle can be attached to the same holomem simultaneously -- each one will activate its effect. When combined, they can deal a significant amount of damage.In the late game, aim to bloom into your 2nd holomem hBP03-056 Tokoyami Towa. The Arts “Break your ×××” increases or decreases in effect depending on the number of back holomem with #Singing, so use this Arts when you have 4 back holomem with #Singing. If damage is still lacking, use the Oshi skill “Devil’s Voice” to down your opponent’s holomem.

key_cards.name: Tokoyami Towa【1st】
key_cards.text: She has a very powerful ability that deals special damage through her collab effect. In addition, her strong synergy with the mascot hBP03-101 Bibi and the tool hBP03-096 Rifle allows her to deal significant damage from the early game with just 1 cheer.

key_cards.name: Bibi
key_cards.text: The Tokoyami Towa Deck includes many holomem with relatively low HP, but the +20 HP effect from hBP03-101 Bibi comes in handy here. The boost to special damage can be effective throughout the game, so make sure to attach it when the time is right.

strategy.text: In the early game, set up your Debut holomem on the stage and prepare so that you can bloom into your 1st holomem on the following turn. By collabing hBP03-062 Inugami Korone with cheer attached and activating the collab effect “Korone Diner”, you can add a Debut holomem from your deck, so make active use of this combo as early as possible.When you collab “Inugami Korone” with hBP03-102 Futoinu attached, you can send cheer from your archive back onto “Inugami Korone”. Reuse the archived cheer with “Korone Diner” and use it to activate Arts.In the mid game, strengthen your hand and stage using support cards such as hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka and hSD01-017 Maager-chan.hBP03-063 Inugami Korone has impressive durability with 200 HP and can use her Arts with just 1 yellow cheer. Until your setup is complete, deal damage with hBP03-063 Inugami Korone while enduring your opponent’s Arts. hBP01-114 Stone Axe increases the damage you can deal, so attach it whenever available. Try to bring your opponent’s 2nd holomem down to approximately 170–200 HP.In the late game, bloom into your 2nd holomem hBP03-066 Inugami Korone. If your opponent’s holomem is a 2nd holomem, hBP03-066 Inugami Korone can deal 170 damage by archiving 1 of the 1st holomem cards stacked under her. If you have hBP03-102 Futoinu and hBP01-114 Stone Axe attached, the damage increases to 200, so actively aim for a knockout when you want to take down your opponent’s 2nd holomem.When hBP03-066 Inugami Korone is knocked down, she can send 1 cheer from your cheer deck to Inugami Korone. Therefore, try to prepare a 1st holomem Inugami Korone in your back position ahead of time to be able to launch a smooth counterattack on your next turn.

key_cards.name: Inugami Korone
key_cards.text: When your 2nd holomem hBP03-066 Inugami Korone is downed, you can use the SP Oshi skill “Wow Wow Wow Wow Wow” to move the cheer attached to the downed Inugami Korone onto another Inugami Korone while also returning hBP03-066 Inugami Korone to your hand. Once hBP03-066 Inugami Korone is back in your hand, she can be brought back onto the stage on your next turn for another round of action.

key_cards.name: Inugami Korone【1st】
key_cards.text: “Boxing Style” can be used when this card is in your collab position.With “Boxing Style”, the “Inugami Korone” in your center position is protected from special damage and changes to HP during your opponent’s main step. When you have a 2nd holomem “Inugami Korone” in your center position and want to avoid her being downed, aiming for a collab is an effective strategy.

strategy.text: In the early game, prepare Debut holomem on your stage while using support cards like hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka to organize your hand. Once you have 2 holo Power accumulated, use the SP Oshi skill “Watame isn’t at fault, right?” to send 2 cheer to hBP03-069 Tsunomaki Watame or hBP03-070 Tsunomaki Watame, then start activating Arts that require multiple cheer right from the early game.hBP03-072 Tsunomaki Watame truly shines when she has 6 or more cheer -- there is no need to rush until you’re ready to go on the offensive.In the mid-to-late game, take your time accumulating cheer and focus on using Arts from 1st holomem to target your opponent’s holomem for downs. Attaching hBP03-112 Watamates to “Tsunomaki Watame” with multiple cheer is a safe strategy, as it allows you to transfer the attached cheer to another “Tsunomaki Watame” when she is downed. Each hBP03-112 Watamates works independently, so consider attaching 2 or more “Watamates” for maximum effect.When hBP03-072 Tsunomaki Watame has 6 cheer attached and can use Arts, even from the collab position, the deck truly comes into its own.In the late game, activate hBP03-072 Tsunomaki Watame’s Arts “Watame Night Fever!!” and combine with collab holomem to deal massive damage -- aim for a series of downs, if possible. By using hBP01-108 So, That Makes You My Enemy and hBP03-088 Totsumachi, you can overwhelm your opponent by downing holomem in both the center and collab positions.

key_cards.name: Tsunomaki Watame【1st】
key_cards.text: Even as a 1st holomem, she has HP comparable to a 2nd holomem. By combining the Arts “You'll cheer me on, right?” with the SP Oshi skill “Watame isn’t at fault, right?”, you can activate it as early as turn 2 to deal 90 damage, making it an exceptionally powerful play.

key_cards.name: Watamates
key_cards.text: Transferring the cheer from a downed “Tsunomaki Watame” to another “Tsunomaki Watame” and ultimately connecting 6 or more cheer onto the 2nd holomem hBP03-072 Tsunomaki Watame is the core strategy of this deck. Make active use of hBP03-112 Watamates to ensure that the cheer on your stage doesn’t decrease.

strategy.text: At the early stages of the game, start by placing Debut holomem on the stage, and use cards like hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka to set up your hand. This allows you to progress faster than your opponent. When using hBP02-080 holoX, be careful not to add more Debut holomem than necessary.Begin blooming your Debut holomem into 1st holomem. Since the bloom effect of hBP05-060 Takane Lui allows you to prepare your hand without using LIMITED support cards, you should proactively bloom your holomem. If you can't draw a card, utilize the Oshi skill to search for key support cards or hBP02-040 Sakamata Chloe.Once you bloom into hBP02-040 Sakamata Chloe, it's time to go on offensive mode! Since this deck has many holomem, you can deal significant damage with "holoX-lot" on every turn. If you successfully return multiple Debut holomem to the bottom of the deck with hBP02-080 holoX, you can continuously reduce your opponent's life with the gift ability.

key_cards.name: Sakamata Chloe【2nd】
key_cards.text: The key card of this deck.If all cards revealed by the Arts share the same bloom level, deal 160 damage and definitively reduce the opponent's life by 1. Even if you can't down the opponent's holomem, reducing their life by 1 is still possible with a matching bloom level, allowing you to win purely through luck.

key_cards.name: holoX
key_cards.text: This card increases the deck's stability.In addition, by returning cards you do not add to your hand to the bottom of the deck in any order, you can increase the success rate of holoX-lot in the late game.

strategy.text: Start by using hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka and hBP02-084 Mikkorone 24 to set up your hand, while hBP01-104 Normal PC helps you search for Debut holomem to place on the stage.Ideally, you’ll want to have two or more Debut Houshou Marine and one hBP01-038 Usada Pekora on your stage.In the mid-game, use the bloom effect of hBP02-032 Houshou Marine to add your preferred Houshou Marine from the deck to your hand and prepare for the end of the game.The 2nd Houshou Marine shines in the end game, gaining power based on the number of holomem stacked beneath her. To protect her from being taken down by your opponent, keep her in the back position as she blooms.For your center position, place a holomem with high HP, such as hBP02-030 Houshou Marine, and make good use of Arts.Towards the end of the game, use bloom on the 2nd holomem, hBP02-033 Houshou Marine. Return a holomem from your archive to your hand to set up your next move, and when there are three or more holomem stacked, you can deal damage to your opponent’s holomem with special damage.Additionally, use the SP Oshi skill “Set Sail~!” to deal heavy damage and pressure your opponent’s life.

key_cards.name: Houshou Marine【1st】
key_cards.text: The core card that provides stability to the Houshou Marine deck. By continuously blooming this card, you can maximize the effects of the 2nd holomem, hBP02-033 Houshou Marine, as well as your SP Oshi skill.

key_cards.name: Dokuro-kun
key_cards.text: A mascot that increases Arts damage and, when bloomed in the center position, lets you draw a card. Bloom the holomem it’s attached to and draw a card, then search for hBP02-032 Houshou Marine, the holomem that kickstarts your offense.

strategy.text: Start by using hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka and hBP02-084 Mikkorone 24 to set up your hand, while hBP01-104 Normal PC helps you search for Debut holomem to place on the stage.Ideally, you’ll want two or more Debut Pavolia Reine and at least one hBP01-012 Airani Iofifteen on your stage.In the mid-game, use the bloom effect of hBP02-022 Pavolia Reine to search your deck for hBP02-022 Tatang and attach it to a holomem, while using the bloom effect of hBP01-054 Airani Iofifteen to send cheers to your holomem. Actively use Arts to down your opponent’s holomem.Towards the end of the game, aim to bloom the 2nd holomem, hBP02-023 Pavolia Reine. Sending missing colors of cheers with its bloom effect increases Arts damage. When holomem are down and their cheers are sent to the archive, use the collab effect of hBP01-055 Airani Iofifteen to send cheers from the archive, and continue using Arts to keep your offense up. Once you have enough cheers on the stage, use the SP Oshi skill “Vibrant Banquet” to down your opponent’s holomem all at once.

key_cards.name: Pavolia Reine【2nd】
key_cards.text: This holomem can recover up to 50 HP and, with its Arts ability, sends cheers from the archive. It is very powerful, but because it has a low HP, be careful not to have it downed on your opponent’s next turn when using Arts.

key_cards.name: Tatang
key_cards.text: This holomem has a very high HP-boosting effect. The holomem it’s attached to becomes harder to be downed, so ideally attach it to holomem placed in the center or collab positions whenever possible.

strategy.text: At the early stages of the game, prepare your hand with hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka and hBP-084 Mikkorone 24, while searching for Debut holomem with hBP01-104 Normal PC and putting them onto the stage. Furthermore, collab with holomem, who have hBP02-089 Oruyanke, will make them harder to down due to the HP boost effect, and you can also draw a card.In the mid-game, increase the number of mascots on stage while searching for #Shirakami's Character using the bloom effect of hBP02-011 Shirakami Fubuki or the Oshi skill "Mascot Creation", and deal damage to your opponent's holomem with hBP02-010 Shirakami Fubuki. Mascots that are sent to the archive when the holomem they were attached to is downed can be returned to your hand using the ability of hBP02-091 Fubu-chun.Towards the end of the game, aim to bloom into the 2nd holomem, hBP02-013 Shirakami Fubuki. She possesses an Arts that significantly increases based on the number of mascots on the stage, allowing her to deal massive damage to your opponents' holomem. When you down your opponent's holomem, aim for the additional damage effect from the SP Oshi skill "FBKINGDOM" to gain an advantage.

key_cards.name: Shirakami Fubuki【1st】
key_cards.text: During the game, she can search your deck for the mascots best suited for your game. You should utilize her effectively by using hBP02-090 Nejimakitsune to make good use of your cheer on holomem who are about to be downed, defending with hBP02-093 Miteiru against opponents who target your back position with special damage, and using hBP02-092 Fubzilla when you want to deal huge Arts damage.

key_cards.name: Fubzilla
key_cards.text: A card that significantly boosts Arts damage in exchange for your cheer. Fubzilla allows you to down high-HP Buzz holomem in a single hit, or deal significant damage even with a 1st holomem on turns when you can't prepare a 2nd holomem.

strategy.text: At the early stages of the game, deploy Debut holomem onto the stage and use cards like hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka and hSD01-017 Manager-chan to prepare your hand.Proceed to bloom your Debut holomem into 1st holomem.Prepare your hand with hBP02-057 Mori Calliope and increase the archive with hBP02-088 Mori Calliope's Scythe.Return necessary holomem to your hand using the Oshi skill of hBP02-007 Mori Calliope.Bloom into hBP02-059 Mori Calliope, then attack.If you have eight or more holomem with #Myth in your archive, you can deal enormous damage.Once you are ready, use the SP Oshi skill of hBP02-007 Mori Calliope to use the Arts twice and down multiple holomem.

key_cards.name: Mori Calliope【2nd】
key_cards.text: This is the key card of this deck.She allows you to archive any card you like from your deck.You can archive hBP02-088 Mori Calliope's Scythe and return it to your hand with hBP02-058 Mori Calliope, or archive hBP02-059 Mori Calliope and return her to your hand with the Oshi skill of hBP02-007 Mori Calliope.

key_cards.name: Mori Calliope【1st】
key_cards.text: She is a necessary card to power up the Arts of hBP02-059 Mori Calliope.Bloom assertively to increase your archive.

strategy.text: In the early game, deploy Debut holomem onto the stage and use cards like hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka and hBP02-081 hololive Indonesia Gen 2 to prepare your hand.Proceed to bloom your Debut holomem into 1st holomem.Prepare your hand with hBP02-052 Kureiji Ollie and hBP02-097 UDIN.hBP02-051 Kureiji Ollie allows you to bloom using holomem from the archive.Bloom into hBP02-053 Kureiji Ollie, then attack.If you have one more hBP02-053 Kureiji Ollie, the Arts damage will increase, allowing you to deal even more substantial Arts damage.If she is downed, revive her like a zombie using the SP Oshi skill of hBP02-006 Kureiji Ollie.

key_cards.name: Kureiji Ollie【2nd】
key_cards.text: She is a holomem who can deal huge damage by archiving two cards from your hand.Use the Oshi skill of hBP02-006 Kureiji Ollie to bloom the holomem on the stage with the holomem you archived.

key_cards.name: Kureiji Ollie【1st】
key_cards.text: She enables a bloom using a holomem from the archive.Bloom using the holomem archived by hBP02-049 Kureiji Ollie or hBP02-097 UDIN.

strategy.text: Start by deploying Debut holomem to the stage, while using hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka and hBP02-084 Mikkorone 24 to set up your hand to get yourself ready before your opponent.Bloom your Debut holomem into your 1st holomem. Use the bloom effect of hBP02-045 Murasaki Shion to add hBP02-047 Murasaki Shion and hBP02-038 Sakamata Chloe to your hand, and actively bloom whenever possible.Once you can bloom into hBP02-047 Murasaki Shion, begin your attack. Use its bloom effect to move your opponent’s cheers and increase Arts damage to take them down all at once. By also using the SP Oshi skill of hBP02-005 Murasaki Shion, you may be able to down two of your opponent’s holomem.

key_cards.name: Murasaki Shion
key_cards.text: A card that maximizes the strengths of your holomem Murasaki Shion.Its Oshi skill can increase the success rate of hBP02-043 Murasaki Shion and hBP05-047 Murasaki Shion, while its SP Oshi skill can down both your opponent’s center holomem and collab holomem at the same time, making it effective throughout the start to the end of the game.

key_cards.name: Murasaki Shion【2nd】
key_cards.text: The key card of this deck. It can manipulate your opponent’s cheers and increase the damage of its own Arts, making it useful in many situations. It also pairs well with the SP Oshi skill of hBP02-005 Murasaki Shion, so use it actively whenever possible.

strategy.text: Start by placing Debut holomem on stage as you assemble your hand using support cards like hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka and hSD01-017 Manager-chan.Using hBP01-104 Normal PC to place hBP01-038 Usada Pekora or hBP01-039 Usada Pekora on stage will help your mid-game run more smoothly.Bloom your Debut holomem into their 1st holomem. The bloom effect of hBP01-041 Usada Pekora allows you to send cheers to your holomem, making it easier to use Arts in the latter half of the game.hBP01-109 Tale of the Moon and the Rabbit is useful for adding 1st and/or 2nd holomem to your hand.In the latter half of the game, bloom into hBP01-043 Usada Pekora and aim for massive damage to your opponent's holomem. Even if you roll a low number when using Arts, you can reroll the die using hBP01-004 Usada Pekora’s SP Oshi skill or hBP01-123 Wild Rabbit Alliance, so make the most of them to secure your victory.

key_cards.name: Usada Pekora【2nd】
key_cards.text: The higher the number rolled on the die, the greater the damage dealt – up to a maximum of 240.With lady luck on your side, this card can deal huge damage in one go.

key_cards.name: Wild Rabbit Alliance
key_cards.text: A support card for hBP01-043 Usada Pekora.It resets the rolled result and lets you roll the die once more, giving it a vital role in the deck.

strategy.text: Start by placing Debut holomem on the stage as you assemble your hand using support cards like hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka and hBP01-102 Idol Microphone.If you are the second player, you can use Arts from your first turn. Combine it with hBP01-010 Amane Kanata’s collab effect to deal substantial damage.Bloom your Debut holomem into their 1st holomem. Deal damage to your opponent's center holomem with hBP01-013 Amane Kanata while aiming to down them using Arts. 2nd hBP01-014 Amane Kanata will come in handy toward the end of the game, but her Arts require three cheers, so be sure to allocate your cheers wisely.Towards the end of the game, bloom into 2nd hBP01-014 Amane Kanata. If the damage you deal exceeds your opponent’s remaining HP by 50 or more, you can reduce one extra of their life. By using the Oshi skill, Squish Squish, you can ensure extra damage is dealt. Turn the tables by reducing your opponent's life in one go!

key_cards.name: Upao
key_cards.text: Has an ability that reflects 20 damage when taking damage. Can also be brought in with hBP01-012 Amane Kanata’s bloom effect, and combined with the +10 Arts damage, makes it easier to down your opponent’s holomem.

key_cards.name: Amane Kanata【Debut】
key_cards.text: Wields an exceptional ability that adds 30 damage to the center holomem’s Arts with the collab effect. This powerful boost enables the Amane Kanata Deck to down the opponent’s holomem with Arts even in the very early turns of the game.

strategy.text: Start by placing Debut holomem on stage as you assemble your hand using support cards like hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka and hSD01-017 Manager-chan.If you have hBP01-104 Normal PC, search a hBP01-057 Takane Lui and get a head start by dealing special damage.Bloom your Debut holomem into their 1st holomem. The bloom effect for hBP01-060 Takane Lui, It's All Thanks to You Guys!, allows you to draw two cards from your deck by archiving one card from your hand. When you use your Oshi holomem skill, Female Commanding Officer here, you can archive your holoPower instead of a card from your hand, which is perfect when you do not want to reduce your hand.Using hBP01-051 Kazama Iroha's Arts, A Bundle of Cheers, while she is in the collab position, will add 20 damage to the Arts for every cheer card she holds. Being able to deal high damage from the start with few cheer cards can give you the upper hand by downing your opponent's Debut holomem. When hBP01-051 Kazama Iroha receives 120-130 damage, bloom her to hBP01-050 Kazama Iroha to prevent your life from being reduced by two at one go.Towards the end of the game, collab hBP01-050 Kazama Iroha on every turn and use the gift, Bodyguard, to protect your center holomem. Place hBP01-061 Takane Lui as your center holomem and use the Arts, Haweye, to deal damage to your opponent's center holomem.The bloom effect for hBP01-061 Takane Lui, Organization Command Center, is a convenient ability that allows you to return up to two archived holomem to your hand. Even if the holomem you want to bloom is in your archive, you can return it to your hand.

key_cards.name: Kazama Iroha【1st】
key_cards.text: The gift, Bodyguard, is a fantastic ability that is useful in any situation. It protects your center holomem from your opponent's Arts, so you should actively collab them if you have a center holomem that you do not want downed.

key_cards.name: Takane Lui【2nd】
key_cards.text: You can return holomem with the tag #holoX from your archive to hand using the bloom effect, Organization Command Center.You can return your downed hBP01-050 Kazama Iroha to your hand and place it in the collab position, or return hBP01-061 Takane Lui to increase your hand.You can strategize by using a card that you want to return to your hand later on when archiving a card for using the Arts, Hawkeye. With your Oshi holomem's Oshi skill, Female Commanding Officer, you can also choose to archive a holoPower instead of your hand!

strategy.text: Start by placing Debut holomem on stage as you assemble your hand using support cards like hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka and hBP01-102 Idol Microphone.If you have hBP01-104 Normal PC, you can search any Hoshimachi Suisei or hBP01-088 Moona Hoshinova and place them on stage. If you have hBP01-077 Hoshimachi Suisei with a cheer, collab with her to draw 2 cards using the collab effect.Bloom your Debut holomem into their 1st holomem. The bloom effect for hBP01-090 Moona Hoshinova, Lapis Lazuli, allows you to send a cheer to your holomem. This deck requires you to archive many cheer cards, so the strategy is to create as many opportunities as possible to send cheers to your holomem.hBP01-079 Hoshimachi Suisei can deal 20 damage to your opponent's back holomem. If your opponent is preparing their 2nd holomem, dealing 20 damage beforehand will allow hBP01-081 Hoshimachi Suisei to down that holomem more easily. Additionally, another strategy is to use hBP01-007 Hoshimachi Suisei's Oshi skill, Comet, together to deal 70 damage and down your opponent's Debut holomem.Towards the end of the game, bloom hBP01-081 Hoshimachi Suisei and use the Arts Shining Comet to down your opponent's holomem. With the collab effect from hBP01-081 Hoshimachi Suisei, you can send one cheer card, so you can send it to the same holomem, or to another holomem that can use Arts in the next turn. When you collab, they will rest on the next turn, so you should always make sure to have two or more hBP01-081 Hoshimachi Suisei on stage.

key_cards.name: Hoshimachi Suisei
key_cards.text: The SP Oshi skill, Shooting Star, is an ability in which your blue holomem deals the same damage to your opponent's back holomem as you did to your opponent's center or collab holomem. You can reduce 2 of your opponent's life, and down the holomem that your opponent would have used in their next turn.

key_cards.name: Amazing PC
key_cards.text: A useful card when you are unable to draw hBP01-081 Hoshimachi Suisei from your deck. You’ll need to archive one of your cheers, but by archiving a cheer from a resting holomem, you can efficiently add the holomem you need to your hand.

strategy.text: Start by placing Debut holomem on stage as you assemble your hand using support cards like hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka and hSD01-17 Manager-chan.If you have hBP01-104 Normal PC, draw cards like hBP01-063 Takanashi Kiara and Omaru Polka to place them on the stage. With the collab effect of hBP01-063 Takanashi Kiara, add the card hBP01-063 Kotori to your hand.Bloom your Debut holomem into their 1st holomem. Blooming Takanashi Kiara with hBP01-125 Kotori lets you draw a card from your deck, so be sure to make the most of this effect! Additionally, holomem in the center or collab position with hBP01-125 Kotori receive 10 less damage, so use that to your advantage.Use hBP01-071 Omaru Polka to deal damage during the performance phase. The more fans you have on stage, the stronger the Arts will be, so you should place cards like hBP01-125 KFP and hBP01-126 Troupe Member when possible.Towards the end of the game, bloom hBP01-066 Takanashi Kiara and use the Arts, Majestic Pheonix, to down your opponent's holomem. The more holomem you have in your archive, the more damage hBP01-066 Takanashi Kiara can deal, so aim to have more than 10 holomem there.

key_cards.name: Takanashi Kiara
key_cards.text: The SP Oshi skill Rise from the ashes is an incredible ability that reduces life loss by 1 and returns a downed holomem to your hand. Use this skill when your 2nd holomem is downed, then bloom the same card again as your 2nd on the next turn, and continue to deal damage with Arts.

key_cards.name: Takanashi Kiara【1st】
key_cards.text: Using the bloom effect, you can take a look at the top 3 cards of your deck and add one holomem to your hand. The remaining cards can be archived, so you should actively use this effect to increase the number of holomem in your archive. You can use hBP01-006 Takanashi Kiara's Oshi skill, Phoenix Tail, to return archived holomem to your hand.  Archiving holomem at the start of the game is also a useful plan.

strategy.text: Start off by placing Debut holomem on your stage, building up your holoPower through collabs, and using support cards like hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka or hBP01-113 Promise to organize your hand. Ideally, use hBP01-104 Normal PC is one of a good option, to place 1–2 of Nanashi Mumei, IRyS, and Ouro Kronii each on your stage.Mid-game, bloom your Debut holomem into their 1st holomem. With the bloom effect of hBP01-019 Nanashi Mumei, holomem with #Promise can bloom into their 1st holomem. hBP01-030 IRyS wields an extremely powerful Arts on top of her bloom effect, making her a key piece to downing your opponent's holomem.For flexibility, use the SP Oshi skill Amazing Drawing to add hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka to your hand when your options are limited, or hBP01-108 So, That Makes You My Enemy when you want to go on the offensive.Towards the end of the game, bloom hBP01-020 Nanashi Mumei and hSD01-007 IRyS to down your opponent's holomem. Both holomem have Arts that get stronger the more holomem you have on your stage, so aim to keep six holomem on stage whenever possible.If your holomem is about to be downed, protect them with the Oshi skill, Guardian of Civilization.

key_cards.name: Ouro Kronii【1st】
key_cards.text: Use the bloom effect to send cheers to holomem with #Promise. As the Arts for 2nd hBP01-019 Nanashi Mumei and hSD01-007 IRyS require three cheers, the bloom effect is especially helpful for speeding things up.

key_cards.name: I'll smack you with a blunt weapon!
key_cards.text: This event activates only once per game, and only when your Oshi is hBP01-002 Nanashi Mumei.It lets you archive two cheers from your opponent’s center holomem, preventing her from using Arts on her next turn.

strategy.text: Start by placing Debut holomem on stage as you assemble your hand using support cards like hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka and hSD01-017 Manager-chan. By using hBP01-104 Normal PC to place one hBP01-024 Vestia Zeta on stage will help your mid-game flow smoothly.Bloom your Debut holomem into their 1st holomem. Use hBP01-026 Vestia Zeta’s bloom effect to draw and bloom your back holomem at the same time to prepare for what’s ahead, while using hBP01-86 Kobo Kanaeru’s bloom effect to deal damage.Start your offense when you bloom into hBP01-87 Kobo Kanaeru. Make full use of the Arts abilities and Oshi skills to deal damage to your opponent's back holomem. Once you have dealt sufficient damage, use Wave Mantra to pave your way to victory. If hBP01-027 Vestia Zeta is in a collab, it will make your holomem harder to be downed, ensuring safety in your battle.

key_cards.name: Kobo Kanaeru【2nd】
key_cards.text: This deck's key card for area-of-effect damage.Using Wave Mantra after Rain Mantra will allow you to deal immense damage.

key_cards.name: Vestia Zeta【1st】
key_cards.text: A protective card that nullifies your opponent's attack.As hBP01-87 Kobo Kanaeru has low HP, using hBP01-027 Vestia Zeta's Gift ability effectively to protect her will give you the upper hand.

strategy.text: Start off by placing Debut holomem on your stage, collabing holomem to accumulate holoPower, and using the support card hSD01-016 Harusaki Nodoka or hSD01-017 Manager-chan to assemble your hand. If you are the 2nd player, you can use Arts from your first turn. You can use hBP01-114 Stone Axe to deal significant damage, but be careful not to get your holomem downed on the next turn.In mid-game, bloom your Debut holomem into their 1st holomem. Use the Oshi skill, Survival Power, to equip hBP01-114 Stone Axe to your holomem, and use their Arts. As hBP01-034 Aki Rosenthal has a lot of HP, the best move would be to equip hBP01-114 Stone Axe to her. Additionally, placing the Mascot hBP01-119 Jobz on her allows you to restore the damage taken from hBP01-114 Stone Axe and draw a card from your deck, making it a compelling combo.Towards the end of the game, bloom into 2nd hBP01-037 Aki Rosenthal to down your opponent's holomem with a powerful Arts. Restore HP with the SP Oshi skill, Song of the Earth, while dealing damage with Arts repeatedly - and make victory yours!

key_cards.name: Stone Axe
key_cards.text: Although it is usually considered a debuff, it proves to be a useful card when used in the Aki Rosenthal Deck. The Arts +20 ability proves to be extremely advantageous throughout the game, so you should aim to have this card placed on holomem who use Arts.

key_cards.name: Aki Rosenthal【1st】
key_cards.text: With the bloom effect, you can retrieve hBP01-114 Stone Axe from your archive. It is beneficial for games where four copies of hBP01-114 Stone Axe are not enough. Additionally, being able to send cheers to your own holomem when using Arts is another powerful effect.
